title: SRS Type Definition
description: A named collection of FieldAssignments that defines the structure of a TypedRecord or Record. Types belong to a namespace and are versioned.

name.description: Machine-readable name within the namespace, snake_case.

aiGuidance.description: Guidance for AI agents determining whether source material matches this Type.

fields.description: Ordered list of fields that make up this Type.

fieldGroups.description: ext:field-groups — grouped, potentially repeatable field sets.

lifecycle.description: ext:lifecycle — inline state machine declaration. Mutually exclusive with lifecycleRef.

lifecycleRef.description: ext:lifecycle — reference to an installed Lifecycle by id. Mutually exclusive with lifecycle.

extendsTypeId.description: ext:type-inheritance — the UUID of the base Type this Type specializes.

extendsTypeVersion.description: ext:type-inheritance — the version of the base Type being extended.

fieldOrder.description: ext:type-inheritance — explicit ordering for the merged field list (base + own). Must contain every fieldId from the effective field set exactly once.

fieldAssignmentOverrides.description: ext:type-inheritance — per-field overrides applied to inherited FieldAssignments.

identityFieldId.description: RFC-020 — names one fieldId from this Type's effective field set (own fields plus, under ext:type-inheritance, inherited fields) as the record's identity/display field. MUST reference a fieldId present in the effective field set (Rule [N+33]). Under ext:type-inheritance, a Type that declares no identityFieldId of its own inherits the effective identityFieldId of its base Type, resolved transitively up the ancestor chain (Rule [N+34]) — this cascading inheritance is specific to identityFieldId and is not shared with fieldOrder, which is single-level only.

validationRules.description: ext:cross-field-validation — cross-field validation rules applied to Records of this Type.

initialState.description: Must reference a state name with isInitial: true (Invariant 4).

LifecycleState.description: A lifecycle state (VocabularyEntry specialization). key is the unified substrate field (was name pre-RFC-006).

key.description: Machine-readable state key. Unified substrate field (was name).

id.description: Stable UUID identity for this transition edge.

fieldId.description: References a Field by its stable id.

required.description: Whether this field must be populated before a Record can be logged.

repeatable.description: ext:repeatable-fields — whether this field may contain multiple entries.

displayLabel.description: Context-specific label override for this field within this Type.

defaultValue.description: Optional default value to pre-populate the field.

FieldAssignmentOverride.description: ext:type-inheritance — overrides for a single inherited FieldAssignment.

fieldId.description: The fieldId of the inherited field being overridden.

required.description: May tighten (false→true) but not relax (true→false) the base field's required status.

compositeRenderer.description: ext:field-groups RFC-007 — dispatch rendering to a named composite renderer. Bare names (e.g. 'table') are SRS-reserved; vendor values use 'reverse-domain/name'. Unknown values fall back to per-field baseline with a diagnostic [FG-Cx1].

CrossFieldRule.description: ext:cross-field-validation — a constraint that validates a relationship between fields in a Record.

message.description: Optional human-readable description of the rule.

predicateFieldId.description: Field whose value is tested (conditional-required, field-ordering).

predicateValue.description: Value the predicate field must equal to activate the rule (conditional-required).

targetFieldId.description: Field that is constrained when the rule fires (conditional-required, field-ordering).

effect.description: Ordering direction for a field-ordering rule.

fieldIds.description: Fields of which at most one may be non-empty (mutual-exclusion).

CrossFieldRuleEffect.description: Ordering direction for a field-ordering rule: the predicate field must-precede or must-follow the target.
